findingDetails.title: Iteration prompt pack points executor contexts at a non-existent review doctrine path
findingDetails.evidence: The shared prompt pack tells executors to load `.agents/skills/sk-code-review/references/review_core.md`, while the canonical agent loads `.opencode/skills/sk-code-review/references/review_core.md`; the YAML renders the prompt pack and passes it to native and CLI executor routes.
findingDetails.recommendation: Change the prompt-pack doctrine path to `.opencode/skills/sk-code-review/references/review_core.md`, or render a runtime-resolved variable that always points to an existing review_core.md surface before dispatch.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The prompt pack is rendered by deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml and used as native context and CLI Codex stdin; direct file discovery found the canonical .opencode review_core path and no .agents/skills review_core path.

findingDetails.title: Runtime mirrors re-label their packaged mirror path as canonical while command/YAML declares `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` canonical
findingDetails.evidence: The canonical OpenCode agent says path references should use `.opencode/agents/*.md`, and the auto workflow names `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` as the canonical path; mirror files instead state their own runtime directory as the canonical runtime path reference while also saying mirrors are downstream packaging surfaces.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reword mirror Path Convention lines to state that the packaged runtime file is a mirror and `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` remains the canonical source/reference path.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct reads across all four agent definitions show the same contract body and mirror-awareness table, with only the Path Convention line varying by runtime while YAML marks `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` canonical.

traceabilityChecks.notes: Compared canonical OpenCode agent, Claude mirror, Codex TOML mirror, and Gemini mirror; one P2 wording drift found.
Checked sk-deep-review skill and prompt-pack executor guidance; one P1 non-existent review_core path found.
Checked command/YAML canonical agent path, prompt-pack render/use, executor routes, and artifact validation requirements.

noveltyJustification: Two new findings were found in the final cross-runtime pass. Weighted new findings are P1=5 plus P2=1; all weighted findings in this iteration are new, so ratio is 6/6 = 1.0.
edgeCases: Direct artifact writing was unavailable/disabled; command manager should materialize returned recovery-ready markdown, state JSONL, delta JSONL, and strategy update summary.

Strategy contains stale machine-owned blocked entries for earlier deferred agent_cross_runtime passes; active Next Focus and dispatch explicitly required cross-runtime-mirror-consistency, so this iteration proceeded.
P2-004 was kept advisory because YAML still names `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` as canonical.
P1-001 was classified required because the shared prompt pack references a non-existent doctrine path used by executor routes.
ruledOut: No delta-file omission finding opened because YAML and prompt pack both require the delta artifact.
No duplicate finding opened for prior P2-001, P2-002, or P2-003.
No finding opened for the runtime mirror awareness table itself; mirror read-only/downstream wording is consistent.
integrationEvidence: .opencode/commands/deep/assets/deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:666-680 native and CLI executor prompt use

title: Iteration prompt pack points executor contexts at a non-existent review doctrine path

reason: Gate-relevant executor prompt defect: shared prompt pack references a non-existent doctrine path used by native/CLI dispatch context.
Cross-runtime mirror wording drift affects traceability/evidence naming, while command/YAML still keeps `.opencode/agents/deep-review.md` operationally canonical.

notes: Compared canonical and mirror deep-review agent definitions; P2-004 opened.
Checked sk-deep-review skill and prompt pack executor guidance; P1-001 opened.

reason: YAML and prompt pack both explicitly require a per-iteration delta artifact.
Mirror-awareness tables consistently classify runtime mirrors as read-only downstream packaging surfaces.
